value.body:
## Problem
The global issue list fans out the same page query to every selected repository and then merges all returned entries. If the UI asks for 100 items and there are 3 repositories, the merged page can contain up to 300 entries. That breaks infinite-scroll page sizing and can make subsequent cursors inconsistent with the displayed ordering.

## Evidence
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/queries/issues.ts:169` builds each infinite query page with `limit` defaulting to 100.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:626` requests a page from every repository with the same `baseQuery`.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/lib/cycleApiClient.ts:651` returns all merged/sorted entries without trimming to the requested limit.

## Expected
A global issue page should respect `query.limit` across the merged result set, not per repository.

## Suggested fix
Implement merged pagination that fetches enough from each repo, sorts globally, returns at most `limit` entries, and encodes per-repository cursors based on the consumed entries. Add a test with two repositories where each has more entries than the limit.

frontmatter.title: Global issue list pagination can return more than the requested page limit

value.title: Global issue list pagination can return more than the requested page limit
